title: Could 'The Mandalorian and Grogu' restore the 'Star Wars' spark? Watch the electrifying final trailer and decide if this is the way
summary: The latest trailer for 'The Mandalorian and Grogu' has reignited excitement for the Star Wars franchise with its explosive final preview. Fans are debating whether the film can restore the series' former spark.

title: What will happen when our sun starts dying? These 'stellar archaeologists' may have found a clue
summary: Scientists, acting as 'stellar archaeologists,' have discovered fossilized magnetism on long-dead white dwarf stars, potentially revealing insights into stellar evolution. This finding may help explain how stars, including our sun, transform as they age.

title: These 'interstellar glaciers' could give water to young star systems. Could they support alien life, too?
summary: NASA's SPHEREx space telescope discovered widespread water ice in the Cygnus X region, suggesting potential for supporting alien life. The ice, shielded by dust in star-forming areas, could provide water to young star systems.

title: Ancient volcanic ash seen blowing across Mars in new spacecraft images
summary: New images from the Mars Express spacecraft reveal ancient volcanic ash spreading across Mars. This discovery provides insights into the planet's geological history.

title: James Webb Space Telescope peers into a dying star surrounded by mysterious buckyballs: 'The structures we're seeing now are breathtaking'
summary: The James Webb Space Telescope captured detailed images of planetary nebula Tc 1, revealing new insights into the aftermath of a sun-like star's death and the presence of mysterious buckyballs. Researchers described the observed structures as 'breathtaking.'

title: Interstellar invader comet 3I/ATLAS formed in a world much colder than the solar system
summary: Comet 3I/ATLAS, an interstellar visitor, originated in a much colder environment than our solar system. This discovery provides insights into the distant regions where it formed.

title: NASA's TESS spacecraft discovers a weird system of exoplanets unlike anything seen before
summary: NASA's TESS spacecraft discovered a unique exoplanet system called TOI-201, which differs significantly from typical planetary arrangements. The system's unusual configuration challenges existing models of planetary formation.

title: Nebula sparkles in new Hubble snapshot | Space photo of the day for April 23, 2026
summary: Hubble has captured a new image of the Trifid Nebula, showcasing its expansive splendor. This is part of the Space photo of the day for April 23, 2026.

title: Japan's audacious sample-return mission to the Mars moon Phobos has made it to the launch pad
summary: Japan's MMX mission, a complex sample-return project, has reached the launch pad. The mission aims to collect material from Mars' moon Phobos and return it to Earth by 2031 to study the origins of the Martian moons.

title: Our recommended choice as the best beginner mirrorless camera has $400 off, and has just hit its best price of the year at Best Buy
summary: The Sony A7C II mirrorless camera has reached its best price of the year with a $400 discount at Best Buy. Expert Kimberley Lane praised it as ideal for enthusiasts, especially for astrophotography.

title: Starship Progress at the Cape with Hoping to Launch this year
summary: SpaceX is making progress on the first flight of Block 3 for Starship at the Cape, aiming for a launch this year. The development marks a significant step toward the next iteration of the spacecraft.

title: NASA and Axiom react to OIG Report on delays in Next-Generation Spacesuit Program
summary: NASA and Axiom Space have responded to a critical report from NASA's Office of Inspector General highlighting delays in the Next-Generation Spacesuit Program. The report raises concerns about the timeline and funding for the development of new spacesuits.

title: Launch Preview: GPS, Progress, and Starlink missions to launch during busy week
summary: Seven space missions, including GPS, Progress, and Starlink launches, are scheduled for the week of April 20 across four countries using five different launch vehicles. This highlights a busy period for the global space industry.

title: AST SpaceMobile’s BlueBird 7 declared lost following New Glenn launch
summary: AST SpaceMobile's BlueBird 7 satellite was declared lost after the failed launch of Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket. This marks a setback for AST SpaceMobile's satellite broadband ambitions.

title: New Glenn launches on third mission, successfully reuses booster for the first time
summary: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket successfully completed its third mission and reused its booster for the first time, just five months after its second launch. This marks a significant milestone in the development of partially reusable heavy-lift launch vehicles.

title: Vast unveils Astronaut Flight Suit and revolutionary Large Docking Adapter
summary: Vast introduced an Astronaut Flight Suit and a Large Docking Adapter, showcasing a blend of fashion and functionality. The innovations are part of the company's efforts to support future commercial space station operations.

title: Ship 39 and Booster 19 Both Complete Full Engine Static Fires
summary: Ship 39 and Booster 19 have completed full engine static fires as part of the Artemis program. This milestone follows the successful return of Orion.

title: Expedition 74 sees vehicle departures and a spacewalk during March
summary: Expedition 74 crew experienced vehicle departures and conducted a spacewalk in March. The month was marked by significant activities aboard the International Space Station.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9 to launch Starlink satellites, New Glenn to launch with flown booster
summary: SpaceX's Falcon 9 is set to launch Starlink satellites, while Blue Origin's New Glenn will debut with a previously flown booster. Six launches are planned globally this week.

title: Falcon 9 launches CRS NG-24 cargo ship to ISS
summary: SpaceX's Falcon 9 rocket successfully launched the CRS NG-24 cargo mission to the International Space Station (ISS). This follows Northrop Grumman's recent Cygnus S.S. William McCool mission concluding the CRS NG-23 flight.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: TEC Business to Business website reported on a space industry event or development, but no specific details were provided in the given text.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) released a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over its 50-year history. The publication celebrates the agency's milestones and contributions to space exploration.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed for capturing space debris or satellites. This innovation could significantly improve space safety and sustainability.

title: Plato aces space-like tests
summary: The European Space Agency's Plato mission has successfully completed space-like tests, remaining on track for a 2027 launch to search for terrestrial planets.

title: SME Forum 2026 – Join ESA's annual consultation process
summary: ESA invites SMEs and midcaps to participate in its annual SME Forum 2026, including an online preparatory session and an in-person event at ESA Headquarters in Paris on June 8-9, 2026.

title: The perfect place for Celeste’s first navigation signal
summary: Celeste has successfully transmitted its first navigation signal from an optimal location. This milestone marks a significant step in the development of its satellite navigation system.

title: First Proba-3 science: surprisingly speedy solar wind
summary: ESA's Proba-3 mission has generated 57 artificial solar eclipses since July 2025, collecting over 250 hours of high-resolution solar corona data. Findings reveal solar wind structures in the inner corona move 3-4 times faster than previously believed.

title: Register for ESA’s Industry Space Days 2026
summary: Registration is now open for ESA's Industry Space Days 2026, scheduled for 16–17 September at the agency's technical centre in Noordwijk, The Netherlands.

title: Pointing in the (b)right direction
summary: The article discusses advancements in spacecraft pointing technology. It highlights improvements in accuracy and efficiency for space missions.

title: Graphene and lasers for space propulsion
summary: An international research team demonstrated light-based propulsion using graphene aerogels during ESA's parabolic flight campaign in May 2025, showing significant thrust under microgravity conditions. The study, led by Université Libre de Bruxelles and Khalifa University, suggests potential applications for steering solar sails and satellite positioning.

title: Astrobotic Hotfires Engine That Could Power Moon Missions
summary: Astrobotic successfully test-fired a Chakram engine prototype for five minutes at NASA’s Marshall Space Flight Center. The engine could potentially power future Moon missions.

title: Orbital vs. Terrestrial Data Center Cost Analysis
summary: A cost analysis compares the build costs of an orbital data center with a terrestrial one, including opportunity costs due to delays. The study is presented in a Payload Pro article.

title: UNIVITY Raises €27M Series A
summary: UNIVITY has secured €27 million in a Series A funding round led by Blast Club and Expansion Venture Capital, with additional support from Bpifrance's Deep Tech fund.

title: Lawmakers Promise to Reject Proposed NASA Cuts—Again
summary: Lawmakers have vowed to reject proposed budget cuts to NASA, citing concerns that the cuts would hinder the agency's ability to meet presidential directives. This marks a recurring effort to defend NASA's funding against budget proposals.

title: Tracking Sats From the Sea
summary: Satellites provide domain awareness for Navy ships, and some officers suggest the Navy can assist in tracking these satellites. The article discusses potential mutual benefits between naval operations and satellite tracking.

title: ATMOS Space Cargo Raises €25.7M Series A
summary: ATMOS Space Cargo has secured €25.7 million in Series A funding to advance its commercial, institutional, and defense-capable space cargo services. The company plans to transition to regular operational service with its ATMOS WORKS and PHOENIX 3 systems.

title: Payload Field Guide: Golden Dome
summary: Golden Dome, a missile defense program announced in January 2025, has attracted significant interest from companies competing for contracts. The initiative aims to enhance US missile defense capabilities.

title: Opportunities Beyond the Moon Opened by CLPS
summary: NASA plans to launch monthly uncrewed missions to the Moon starting next year under the CLPS program, creating opportunities for various space industry companies beyond lunar-focused firms.

title: Economics of Orbital Data Centers Report: Part 1
summary: The first part of a four-part report analyzes the economics of orbital data centers, highlighting how terrestrial bottlenecks are pushing compute capabilities into space.

title: NASA’s 2028 Moon Landing Has A Spacesuit Problem, New Report Says
summary: NASA's planned 2028 Moon landing faces delays due to issues with spacesuit development, as reported by the Office of the Inspector General (OIG). The report highlights significant challenges in the timeline for producing the necessary spacesuits.

title: SpinLaunch to Use Equinx Data Centers as Teleport Sites for its Constellation
summary: SpinLaunch will collaborate with Equinx to deploy teleport sites at Equinx's data centers to support its Meridian constellation. The ultra-compact teleports will serve as ground infrastructure for the satellite network.

title: Iridium Continues IoT Subscriber Growth in Q1, Desch Talks NTN Direct Launch and Spectrum
summary: Iridium reported a 2% year-over-year revenue increase in Q1 2026, driven by continued IoT subscriber growth. CEO Matt Desch also discussed plans for NTN direct launch and spectrum discussions.

title: Univity Raises Further Funding for VLEO Constellation for Europe
summary: Univity, a French constellation startup, raised €27 million ($32 million) in Series A funding to develop two demonstration satellites for a Very Low Earth Orbit (VLEO) connectivity constellation in Europe.

title: US Officials See Proposed European Union Space Act as Complicated Overreach
summary: U.S. officials criticize the European Union's draft Space Act, calling it complicated overreach. The proposed legislation aims to create a unified regulatory framework for EU space activities.

title: Australian Airline Jetstar Selects Viasat for IFC for Dreamliner Fleet
summary: Jetstar Airways, an Australian low-cost airline, has chosen Viasat's Amara solution for in-flight connectivity on its Boeing 787 Dreamliner fleet. The Amara system will be installed across 11 Jetstar aircraft.

title: SSC Space and Kuva Space to Collaborate in Nordic Region
summary: SSC Space and Kuva Space have announced a regional collaboration to enhance space-based capabilities across the Nordic region's space value chain. The partnership aims to strengthen various sectors within the space industry.

title: FCC Grants AST SpaceMobile Commercial Authorization for Direct-to-Device Service
summary: The FCC has granted AST SpaceMobile commercial authorization to provide direct-to-device satellite services. This approval allows the company to use spectrum from partner mobile network operators for its operations.

title: Eutelsat Signs New Broadcast Deals in Mexico and Caribbean
summary: Eutelsat has signed three new broadcast deals in Mexico and the Caribbean, partnering with Co-op Cable to expand direct-to-home (DTH) and connectivity services.

title: Space Capabilities Underpin Global Security Strategies
summary: The article discusses the importance of space capabilities in global security strategies, highlighting insights from SATShow Week and upcoming Space Symposium.

title: Pentagon to Move Forward On AEP Upgrades for GPS, As DoD Cancels GPS OCX
summary: The Pentagon has canceled the GPS Next Generation Operational Control System (OCX) by RTX and will instead upgrade the existing ground system for GPS. The decision involves moving forward with Architecture Evolution Plan (AEP) upgrades for GPS.
